channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Alejandro Welter liderará la estrategia y el desarrollo de mercado de Allianz Seguros
item.description: Allianz Seguros ha nombrado a Alejandro Welter Skala nuevo director de Estrategia y Desarrollo de Mercado, un cargo que asumirá el próximo 1 de septiembre y desde el que impulsará iniciativas para el crecimiento y posicionamiento de la compañía. Además, se incorporará al Comité de Dirección y reportará directamente al consejero delegado, Veit Stutz.

item.title: Generali define mejoras en producto, operativa y digitalización
item.description: Generali ha celebrado una nueva reunión de su consejo consultivo de corredores, un foro de trabajo en el que la aseguradora y representantes de la Mediación analizan iniciativas para impulsar el desarrollo del negocio conjunto.

item.title: Anagan integra la correduría andaluza Marh Underwriting
item.description: Anagan integra la correduría andaluza Marh Underwriting

item.title: Duck Creek Acquires Send, Creating Agentic Underwriting-to-Core Platform
item.description: Duck Creek’s Agentic AI Platform will extend and accelerate Send’s underwriting orchestration capabilities by embedding insurance-native agents, governance and core system connectivity into the underwriting life cycle while giving carriers a foundation to deploy AI across underwriting with greater speed, control and business impact. Send will continue to be available as a standalone underwriting orchestration engine with integration with all core systems, while benefiting from continued investment and deeper integration with Duck Creek’s Intelligent Insurance Cloud and Agentic AI Platform for combined customers. “By bringing Send into Duck Creek, we are combining core insurance operations and underwriting orchestration in a trusted agentic AI experience that connects systems of record, decision intelligence, workflows, and action.

item.title: Corporate Venture Capital Is Splitting In Two
item.description: Two corporate venture programs shutting down inside six weeks invites speculation that corporations are retreating from venture capital, but in fact the opposite is true. According to Bain Capital , corporate investors participated in 68% of global AI deal value in 2025 — venture’s strongest funding year since 2021. Amid this strength, though, corporate venture is also quietly splitting in two, and the proof is buried inside the record numbers.

item.title: Fintech Funding Surges 23% In H1 2026 As Investors Concentrate Their Bets On AI And Financial Infrastructure
item.description: Venture funding into fintech startups climbed nearly 23% year over year in H1 2026, even as deal count fell more than 25%, Crunchbase data shows, a sign that investors are writing fewer, but much larger checks into the sector as they focus on areas such as wealth management, financial infrastructure and enterprise automation. (It’s important to note that H2 2025 marked the strongest six-month funding period for fintech startups since the second half of 2022.) Fintech funding in the first half of 2026 also topped the sector’s investment totals in 2020 and the pre-pandemic year of 2019, though they remain lower than the peak funding year of 2021 as well as 2018. “2026 marks the definitive ‘lab-i-fication’ of the modern corporation,” she noted, with some fintech platforms using their scale and steady profits to fund experimental new divisions.

item.title: Gallagher Re estimates global insured cat losses at $46bn for H1’26, 28% below 10-year average
item.description: Insured losses from natural catastrophe events across the globe in the first half of 2026 are estimated to have reached USD $46 billion, down on last year’s $84 billion and 28% below the 10-year average of $64 billion, according to reinsurance broker Gallagher Re’s H1 2026 Natural Catastrophe and Climate Report. In addition, Gallagher Re noted that economic losses also fell in the first half of 2026 to $142 billion, which is 10% below the 10-year average. However, while global catastrophe losses in H1’26 have remained below average, Gallagher Re highlights the continuing evolution of global risk, highlighted by unprecedented early summer heat in Europe, and the emergence of El Niño conditions during June.

item.title: ILS provides crucial portfolio diversification ahead of Super El Niño: VP Bank’s Allgäuer
item.description: Whilst it appears that a super El Niño could be on the horizon, a new commentary from VP Bank AG authored by Senior Investment Strategist Bernhard Allgäuer indicates that insurance-linked securities (ILS) can provide portfolio diversification as their returns are driven by natural disasters, rather than financial market turmoil, making them largely uncorrelated with traditional asset classes. “Currently, probability forecasts from the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) suggest that a super El Niño could be on the horizon from September 2026. Importantly, Allgäuer emphasises how ILS can provide diversification, even during exceptional market events.

item.title: Unlocking capital markets to close the protection gap demands standardised infrastructure: Montauk Point’s Michel
item.description: This gap is reflected in translation, innovation, and structural barriers between a highly specialised insurance industry and capital markets that demand transparency and standardised infrastructure. In the paper, Michel argues that the protection gap continues to widen not because capital is scarce, but because the nature of risk and economic value is changing faster than insurance and capital markets can adapt. As the global protection gap continues to widen, closing it is no longer a question of mobilising more capital, it is a matter of engineering entirely new markets.

item.title: Cedents increasingly seek reinsurers with access to multiple capital sources: Guy Carpenter’s Paretchan
item.description: The growth of alternative capital is giving buyers more flexibility to build diversified reinsurance programmes, with cedents increasingly seeking partners able to access both traditional and alternative sources of capacity, Guy Carpenter’s Jennifer Paretchan recently told Reinsurance News. Paretchan continued, “What that means for buyers is an opportunity to supplement their traditional programs with other sources and create a more diversified suite of capital when they’re thinking about reinsurance. Speaking following the release of the firm’s July 2026 reinsurance renewal report, Paretchan noted the expansion of dedicated alternative reinsurance capital had become one of the defining themes of the mid-year renewals.

item.title: Arch Insurance International promotes Sophie Law to Cyber Underwriting Manager
item.description: Arch Insurance International, part of Arch Capital Group and a provider of specialty insurance solutions across multiple lines, has promoted Sophie Law to Cyber Underwriting Manager, effective immediately. Barrett commented in the promotion,”Sophie has built a reputation for her underwriting excellence and developing trusted relationships with brokers and clients in a dynamic and fast-evolving cyber market. She will oversee the underwriting activities across the Cyber portfolio, spanning cyber, tech errors & omissions (E&O) and media E&O across a broad range of territories and sectors.

item.title: AM Best affirms stable outlook for the global cyber insurance segment
item.description: AM Best, the global credit rating agency and information provider specialising in the insurance industry, has confirmed a stable outlook on the global cyber insurance segment in it’s latest Market Segment Report, Market Segment Outlook: Global Cyber Insurance. Commenting on the findings, Cristian Sieira, Senior Financial Analyst at AM Best, added: “Our stable outlook on the segment reflects solid demand for coverage, even as the market pricing softens, in addition to favourable profitability over the intermediate terms and the growing use of AI.” Set against these positives, AM Best identifies a set of countervailing pressures. Todor Kitin, Associate Director at AM Best, said: “Insurers remain valuable targets of cyber-attacks due to the concentration of sensitive policyholder and exposure data, increasing their own operational and aggregation risk.” He further added: “Ongoing geopolitical uncertainty is also contributing to an increased cyber threat environment.” On the regulatory side, AM Best observes that data protection obligations have been a significant driver of cyber insurance purchases.

item.title: Russian Strike on Ukraine’s Odesa Kills Three as Moscow, Kyiv Battle Over Black Sea
item.description: Russia and Ukraine stepped up their battle over the Black Sea and key trade routes on Wednesday, with Moscow killing three people in an attack on the Ukrainian port city of Odesa and Kyiv’s drone forces striking Russian shipping. Three people were killed and at least three more injured after a Russian missile strike on a seven-story residential building in Odesa, Ukrainian authorities said. Read more: War Overtakes Civil Unrest as Top Political Violence Exposure for Global Businesses Russia in recent days has stepped up attacks on Ukraine’s deepwater Black Sea ports in the Greater Odesa area , which h andle much of the country’s grain and other cargo and are vital to its wartime economy.

item.title: Update: After Hormuz, Iran Turns to Red Sea Gateway as New Pressure Point
item.description: Having choked off shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, Iran is now signaling it could play its most dangerous card yet: using Yemen’s Houthi allies to shut the Bab el-Mandeb gateway to the Red Sea, opening a new front against Washington and putting two of the world’s most vital energy arteries at risk. strikes deepen inside Iran and Houthi attacks escalate in tandem, analysts say Tehran is widening the conflict and seeking to increase pressure on Washington by extending the threat to global trade and energy supplies beyond the Gulf. Iran has already demonstrated the power of its most valuable strategic asset by disrupting traffic through Hormuz.

item.title: Business Moves: Duck Creek Buys Send to Create Novel Agentic Solution
item.description: Duck Creek said together the companies will create the “first and only agentic solution to unite core insurance operations and intelligent underwriting workflows across the policy lifecycle for multiple operating models.” Insurers will be able to connect submission intake, triage, enrichment, risk assessment, quote decisions, policy issuance, and portfolio monitoring in a more unified operating model—helping underwriters act with greater speed, consistency, and confidence for profitable growth. “By bringing Send into Duck Creek, we are combining core insurance operations and underwriting orchestration in a trusted agentic AI experience that connects systems of record, decision intelligence, workflows, and action. Duck Creek said this platform will extend and accelerate Send’s underwriting orchestration capabilities by embedding insurance-native agents, governance, and core system connectivity into the underwriting lifecycle while giving carriers a foundation to deploy AI across underwriting with greater speed, control, and business impact.

item.title: Cover Genius secures $100 million funding to accelerate AI and embedded insurance growth
item.description: The post Cover Genius secures $100 million funding to accelerate AI and embedded insurance growth appeared first on Insurtech Insights . The company reported: 50% year-over-year revenue growth More than  US$3 billion  in cumulative gross written sales Delivery of over  240 million insurance policies Its embedded protection platform is integrated with major global digital brands, including: Klarna Revolut Priceline Agoda Booking.com Turkish Airlines Uber Amazon eBay Wayfair Flipkart Shopee Cover Genius also said its  XCover  claims platform holds an  “Excellent” 4.5 out of 5 Trustpilot rating , based on more than  70,000 verified customer reviews . Embedded insurance provider Cover Genius has raised US$100 million in new capital from Vista Credit Partners , valuing the company at US$1.9 billion as it looks to expand its AI capabilities, deepen enterprise partnerships and pursue strategic acquisitions.

item.title: Corgi launches Golden to target sports and entertainment insurance market
item.description: Corgi Insurance has launched Golden by Corgi , a dedicated sports and entertainment insurance division designed to provide specialist cover for sports organisations, including leagues, governing bodies, clubs, events and venues. Golden is built on Corgi’s full-stack insurance platform and offers bespoke insurance programmes, rapid certificate issuance, in-house claims handling, embedded risk management support and loss prevention services tailored to the sports sector. The launch marks Corgi’s expansion into a global sports industry valued at more than US$600 billion , with the company aiming to simplify insurance for organisations that have traditionally relied on multiple brokers, carriers and administrators.

item.title: SWBC partners with Covered
item.description: SWBC has partnered with digital insurance platform Covered Insurance to streamline insurance tracking and borrower engagement for mortgage servicers. SWBC will integrate Covered’s technology into its insurance tracking and escrow services to automate insurance monitoring, borrower outreach, issue resolution and compliance documentation. Partnering with Covered reflects the industry’s shift toward more intelligent, automated solutions that enhance visibility, efficiency, and long-term performance.” – Jill Ruggles, Senior Vice President of SWBC Product Management-Mortgage Servicing.

item.title: Anthropic hires Monica Shokrai to lead business risk and insurance
item.description: Anthropic has appointed Monica Shokrai as head of business risk and insurance . Shokrai joins after nearly eight years at Google, where she led business risk and insurance for Google Cloud and headed actuarial, analytics and systems for Alphabet’s insurance team. At Anthropic, Shokrai will oversee risk management and insurance and work with insurers on coverage designed for AI-related risks.

item.title: Erie Insurance expands Hyland partnership
item.description: Erie Insurance is expanding its relationship with enterprise content management provider Hyland through the deployment of its Content Innovation Cloud. “Hyland has been a tremendous partner for Erie Insurance as we evolve into a more human-led, AI-enabled organization focused on better serving our customers. Erie already uses Hyland’s OnBase platform as its core enterprise content management repository.

item.title: After Losing Job and Crypto, Man Falsely Claimed $1.3M From 107 Class Actions
item.description: According to court documents, the man admitted that he used fictitious names to make claims in several hundred class action lawsuits and that he “came up with the idea all by himself.” He told investigators he started doing this after being unemployed due to Covid-19 and following the loss of cryptocurrency. Among the class actions Digennaro is charged with tapping are ones involving Chrysler-Dodge-Jeep, Vimeo, Porsche and EpiPen. An upstate New York man has been accused of receiving more than 27,000 payments totaling more than $1.3 million from approximately 107 different class action lawsuit settlements between January 2022 and December 2025.

item.title: US P/C Industry Books Best Result in a Decade but Not All Lines Enjoy Success
item.description: property/casualty industry posted its best underwriting profit and combined ratio in a decade in 2025, building upon the $1 trillion of total direct premiums written logged the prior year for the first time. According to a new report from industry financial strength rating agency AM Best, DPW grew 5% in 2025 to about $1.11 trillion. “Insurers underwriting both personal auto and homeowners’ lines of coverage have reaped the benefits of technology and data analytics to supplement underwriting, claims handling and ratemaking,” said David Blades, associate director, AM Best.

item.title: Study Explores Social Media’s Role in Tornado Disaster Communication
item.description: But a new study from University of Nebraska–Lincoln media scholar Cory Armstrong found that the same disaster also exposed persistent communication gaps facing rural and underserved communities. By analyzing social media activity before, during and after the tornado, which killed 17 people and injured dozens more, Armstrong and University of Alabama researcher Matthew Van Dyke found that digital platforms helped spread information throughout the disaster. Newswise — When a powerful EF-4 tornado tore through Rolling Fork, Mississippi, on March 24, 2023, social media became a critical tool for sharing weather warnings, damage reports and recovery information.

item.title: AUB Group names Eric Harris as national manager for broker partnerships
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon appoints WTW’s Sean Deehan as APAC Strategy and Technology Group CEO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Asia H1 nat cat insured losses below US$2bn, lowest since 2017: Gallagher Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sompo adds Canon Liu as Head of Financial Lines, Hong Kong, Macau & Taiwan
item.description: Sompo Asia Pacific (Sompo APAC), part of Sompo International Holdings Ltd., has announced the appointment of Canon Liu as Head of Financial Lines, Hong Kong, Macau and Taiwan. Based in Hong Kong, Liu will lead Sompo’s Financial Lines underwriting strategy across the region, driving growth and portfolio expansion across Directors and Officers, Professional Indemnity, Financial Institutions, and Cyber insurance. Liu joins Sompo from QBE Asia, where he led the Financial Lines portfolio across D&O, Professional Indemnity, Crime, Medical Malpractice, and Cyber, setting portfolio strategy and driving profitable growth through disciplined underwriting and commercially focused execution.

item.title: H1’26 insured nat cat losses land 28% below 10-year average at $46bn, says Gallagher Re
item.description: Global insured losses from natural catastrophe events totalled $46 billion in the first half of 2025, down on last year’s $84 billion and 28% below the 10-year average of $64 billion, according to reinsurance broker Gallagher Re’s H1 2026 Natural Catastrophe and Climate Report. In fact, in H1 2026, just 11 events produced insured losses of more than $1 billion, compared with a 10-year average of 16. Economic losses also fell in the first half of the year to $142 billion, which is 10% below the 10-year average.

item.title: Sean Deehan joins Aon as CEO Strategy and Technology Group for APAC
item.description: Aon, a global professional services firm, has appointed Sean Deehan as Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Strategy and Technology Group for Asia Pacific (APAC), effective immediately. “Sean’s regional experience, market knowledge and commercial leadership will help increase our momentum and support continued growth in Asia Pacific.” Deehan added, “Aon’s strategy and technology group has a strong reputation for helping clients make better decisions through innovation and deep industry knowledge. He is based in Hong Kong, and reports to Sherif Zakhary, Global CEO of Aon’s Strategy and Technology Group and Inpoint.

item.title: Markets/Coverages: Lemonade Enters Maine and Vermont Renters Insurance Markets
item.description: Digital insurer Lemonade has expanded availability of its renters insurance into Vermont and Maine. They become the 43rd and 44th states where Lemonade offers renters insurance. We offer simple quotes, instant claims, and rates that don’t break the bank,” said Dan Timsit, head of renters insurance at Lemonade.

item.title: One Weather Firm Warns That New England Could See Big Hurricane This Season
item.description: Murley and Dilley, who’s based in Ocala, Florida, said weather cycle data suggests the hurricane could be as big and as bad as the famous hurricane that struck New England in 1938 and killed more than 680 people. All of the best-known storm forecasting services have predicted a mild 2026 Atlantic hurricane season. But one lesser-known forecaster—whose service was recently bought out by National Weather Forecasting (NWF), a steadily growing forecasting firm, is, so far, sticking to its initial estimate of 13 named storms and four major hurricanes.

item.title: People Moves: Everest Appoints Hanrahan as Global Distribution & Chief Commercial Officer
item.description: , headquartered in Hamilton, Bermuda, appointed Craig Hanrahan as Global Distribution & Chief Commercial Officer, effective August 1, 2026. Hanrahan will lead Everest’s global distribution strategy and key commercial relationships across its insurance and reinsurance businesses, partnering closely with business leaders to advance Everest’s commitment to delivering exceptional service, underwriting expertise and long-term value to clients and brokers worldwide. Since joining Everest in 2024 as Northeast regional executive officer, he also served as head of North America field operations, leading the company’s four U.S.

item.title: BP Marsh homes in on ‘tremendous opportunities’ in APAC insurance distribution niche
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Insurers Must Prepare Now for El Niño Season
item.description: With El Niño forecast at 80% and AI-driven fraud rising, carriers must act now before disaster season arrives. El Niño usually calms the Atlantic hurricane season. The Climate Prediction Center, part of NOAA, recently raised the odds of an El Niño pattern forming to 80%.

item.title: Aegon deploys InfiniteWatch to analyze customer interactions
item.description: Aegon has deployed InfiniteWatch AI Insights to analyze customer interactions and identify technical problems affecting the customer experience. During its first week in production on Aegon’s website, InfiniteWatch analyzed more than 3,000 interactions and turned three previously unexplained issues into actionable tickets. With InfiniteWatch, in the first week, those incidents became three concrete tickets, with evidence and proposed solutions.” – Beatriz Lázaro, Head of Growth at Aegon.

item.title: Curql invests in Wagmo
item.description: Wagmo has received a strategic investment from Curql , a collective of more than 160 credit unions investing in financial technology. “Curql invests in companies that strengthen credit union members’ financial well-being. We’re proud to back Wagmo, which makes pet care simpler and more affordable while thoughtfully positioning itself as both a direct member benefit and a meaningful employee benefit.” – Nick Evens, president and CEO of Curql Collective.

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses Meta of Using AI to Target Workers With Medical Conditions for Layoffs
item.description: Twenty-six former employees of Meta Platforms have filed a lawsuit against the tech company, accusing it of using AI-powered software that disproportionately targeted people with disabilities or who took medical leave in selecting people for mass layoffs. The 26 plaintiffs, who filed the lawsuit anonymously, are accusing Meta of violating federal and state laws that ban discrimination or retaliation against workers who have disabilities, take medical leave or are pregnant. The lawsuit, filed in Oakland, California, federal court late Monday, says that the company relied on factors such as productivity and AI token usage when it began slashing thousands of jobs earlier this year, disadvantaging people who missed work because of medical conditions.

item.title: Keystone Pipeline System’s Operator to Pay $26.9M Penalty Over Kansas Oil Spill
item.description: government would require the Keystone Pipeline system’s operator to pay a $26.9 million civil penalty over a major oil spill in Kansas in December 2022 and spend about $40 million more to prevent future accidents. government, an engineering consulting firm said that a bend in the Keystone system where the spill occurred had been “overstressed” since its installation in December 2010 — likely because construction activity itself altered the land around the pipe. The accident was the largest onshore crude pipeline spill in the U.S.

item.title: Lawsuit Claims Meta Used AI to Target Workers with Medical Conditions for Layoffs
item.description: Twenty-six former employees of Meta Platforms have filed a lawsuit against the tech company, accusing it of using AI-powered software that disproportionately targeted people with disabilities or who took medical leave in selecting people for mass layoffs. The 26 plaintiffs, who filed the lawsuit anonymously, are accusing Meta of violating federal and state laws that ban discrimination or retaliation against workers who have disabilities, take medical leave or are pregnant. The lawsuit, filed in Oakland, California, federal court late Monday, says that the company relied on factors such as productivity and AI token usage when it began slashing thousands of jobs earlier this year, disadvantaging people who missed work because of medical conditions.

item.title: Florida Conducting Annual Slow Down Campaign to Target Speeders
item.description: The state of Florida announced its 9th annual Operation Southern Slow Down campaign targeting speeders across the state, according to a joint announcement by the Florida Department of Transportation (FDOT), the Florida Department of Highway Safety and Motor Vehicles (FLHSMV) and its division of the Florida Highway Patrol (FHP). The multi-state campaign happening across Alabama, Florida, Georgia, South Carolina, and Tennessee, takes place July 13-18, 2026, and focuses on reducing speed-related crashes through a combination of increased enforcement and public education. The choice is simple: slow down, obey the law, and make it home safe.”

item.title: Brute-Force Password Cracking Getting Faster, Longer Passwords Are Better
item.description: Password cracking is a brute-force numbers problem, and the chips designed for AI hold no advantage at it, the cybersecurity company said. New analysis reveals that password cracking becomes 20 to 25% faster every year, according to cybersecurity firm Hive Systems. Measured on the same hashing standard across all three years, the time to brute-force a randomly generated eight-character password using the full mix of characters has fallen from 225 years in 2024 to 164 in 2025 to 132 in 2026 – a drop of roughly 20 to 25% annually.

item.title: ARPC’s claim payouts up marginally in Q1 to surpass AU$1.5bn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Insurance Industry May Be Unprepared for Agentic AI Risks
item.description: New research shows the insurance industry might not be equipped to deal with AI risks. As covered here earlier this month, the insurance industry and regulators have begun pricing AI hallucination risks. While some industry figures say these warnings are overblown, and part of a campaign to push new AI insurance products, AIUC Co-Founder Rajiv Dattani said legal liabilities had held back broader AI use at larger companies.

item.title: David Maslo appointed CEO of African Risk Capacity Ltd
item.description: African Risk Capacity Limited (ARC Ltd.) the financial affiliate and parametric insurance underwriting entity of the African Risk Capacity (ARC) Group, has appointed David Maslo as its permanent CEO, following his successful tenure as interim leader during a period of significant transformation for the company. It will also strengthen the continent’s resilience to climate-related, health and other catastrophic risks, and support Africa’s wider sustainable development agenda.” Commenting on his appointment, David Maslo, Chief Executive Officer of ARC Ltd, said: “It is an honour to lead African Risk Capacity Ltd at this important moment in Africa’s economic development. appointed Maslo as Interim CEO, following his tenure as Chief Risk Management Officer at the Caribbean Catastrophe Risk Insurance Facility Segregated Portfolio Company (CCRIF SPC).

item.title: Reinsurance sidecars remain key in 2026, as third-party capital deployment holds stable: Aon Securities
item.description: Reinsurance sidecars remain a key theme in 2026, with third-party capital deployment holding broadly stable despite a rapidly evolving landscape of perils and structures, according to Aon Securities, the broker-dealer and investment banking arm of the broking firm. “Sidecars remain a major theme in 2026, with overall capital deployment broadly stable and considerable investor interest in asset-driven sidecar vehicles for casualty and whole-account portfolios,” the firm said. Data from the firm showed that reinsurance sidecars reached a new record high of $17 billion as of June 30th 2025 , representing a 70% expansion of this segment of the ILS market in just one year, and marked a notable increased from Aon’s previous estimate that outstanding reinsurance sidecar capital had reached $10 billion at the middle of 2024.

item.title: Active Re closes 2025 with 88.4% CoR and total equity of $108m
item.description: Active Re, a global reinsurer headquartered in Barbados, has released its 2025 Annual Report “Positioned for What’s Ahead”, reporting a combined ratio of 88.4% for 2025, as well as a return on equity of 16.1%, a technical result of USD 26.7 million, and total equity of USD 108 million. Despite a global reinsurance market defined by abundant capital, pricing adjustments across several lines of business, and intensifying competition, Active Re maintained a strategy that prioritises portfolio quality over volume growth. For the third consecutive year, AM Best reaffirmed Active Re’s Financial Strength Rating of A (Excellent) and Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating of “a” (Excellent), both with a stable outlook, highlighting the company’s balance sheet strength, consistent operating performance, and robust enterprise risk management framework.

item.title: Guy Carpenter strengthens LatAm & Caribbean team with Almaraz and Genovese appointments
item.description: Based in Miami, Almaraz and Genovese bring a strong portfolio and deep expertise in health re/insurance that will enhance Guy Carpenter’s health reinsurance capabilities across Latin America and the Caribbean. Both executives join the broker from Worldwide Insurance Associates (WIA) – an international risk management and insurance agency specialising in the Latin American marketplace -, with Almaraz having spent 33 years working for the insurance brokerage Almaraz and Genovese are some of Guy Carpenter’s most recent appointments, following Dennis Sugrue’s as Head of International Business Intelligence. Guy Carpenter, a global reinsurance broker and Marsh business, has announced the appointment of James Almaraz and Robert Genovese as Managing Director and Vice President, respectively.

item.title: Fitch improves Canopius Re outlook to positive while affirming ‘A’ rating
item.description: Credit rating agency Fitch Ratings has upgraded the outlook for Canopius Reinsurance Limited (Canopius Re), the Bermuda-based reinsurance company within the Canopius Group, from Stable to Positive while reaffirming its Insurer Financial Strength (IFS) Rating at ‘A’ (Strong). Fitch also reaffirmed that the rating is supported by Canopius Re’s role as a core operating company within the group, together with the group’s capital position and consistently strong financial performance. While Fitch considers the implied IFS Rating to be ‘A+’, it has maintained the provisional rating at ‘A’.

item.title: Antares’ CEO Michael van der Straaten Dies at 58
item.description: Antares, the specialist global re/insurance group, announced the death of its chief executive officer Michael van der Straaten. “It is with great sadness that we announce that Michael van der Straaten, CEO of Antares, passed away unexpectedly this weekend following a short illness,” according to Antares, the international operations of Qatar Insurance Group (QIC), a major insurer in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. van der Straaten, 58, was named CEO of Antares (formerly named QIC Global ), in 2020.

item.title: AM Best Assigns Credit Ratings to Arkansas’ WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange
item.description: AM Best has assigned a Financial Strength Rating of A- (Excellent) and a Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating of “a-” (Excellent) to WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange (WoodStar), based in Little Rock, Arkansas. In addition, AM Best has assigned a Long-Term Issue Credit Rating of “bbb” (Good) to the $220 million, 9.5% fixed surplus notes, due 2041, issued by WoodStar. The ratings reflect WoodStar’s balance sheet strength, which AM Best assesses as very strong, as well as its adequate operating performance, neutral business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management (ERM).
